title: Scaevola globosa
taxonomy: Scaevola globosa is classified as a species within the family Goodeniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as goodenia or sapphire flowers. The genus Scaevola is a type of shrub or small tree native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Scaevola globosa is specifically a species of Scaevola found in Australia. The classification of Scaevola globosa is further supported by its unique characteristics, including its blue or white flowers and distinctive foliage.

intro: Scaevola globosa, also known as the Australian fan flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia that belongs to the family Goodeniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Scaevola globosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
